Generation Strategy (3 lines):

1. **Azerbaijan-first anchoring**: Every scenario is grounded in the local context — SOCAR subsidiaries, CBA regulations, manat devaluation history (2015 reference), local bank structures — making the questions immediately recognizable and relevant to AZ-based financial professionals rather than generic Western finance cases.

2. **Ethical load balanced across difficulty**: The three hardest questions (prof-fin-003, prof-fin-005, prof-fin-007) combine technical depth with professional integrity pressure — DCF model errors under FX exposure, CFO pressure to shade board reporting, and the 2015 devaluation response — testing whether candidates can reason under real-world constraints, not just recall formulas.

3. **IRT calibration logic**: Easy questions test procedural competence (what to do when you find a data error, how to inherit a model) with discriminating but forgiving parameters (irt_a 0.8–1.0, irt_b −1.5 to −1.2); hard open-ended questions use high discrimination and high difficulty (irt_a 2.1–2.3, irt_b 1.8–2.2) to separate analysts who understand risk frameworks from those who can only name them.
